actionable_feedback: Add separate, machine-readable calculation artifact (calculation_workbook.csv or calculation_workbook.json) that contains the raw packet rows and every intermediate computed column (strength_norm, mass_norm, cost_norm, weighted_contrib, subtotal) so verifiers can fully reproduce numeric results without relying on a truncated markdown preview.

Add a dedicated assumptions_and_sensitivity_notes.md file that: (a) states the raw units used for density and cost (or notes they were treated as packet-relative unitless values), (b) provides a clear manufacturability qualitative->numeric rubric (e.g., easy=1.0, medium=0.5, hard=0.0) and documents whether any provisional mapping/proxies were considered, and (c) includes a short sensitivity table showing how final ranking changes for plausible manufacturability scores (e.g., apply each rubric level to A/B/C and report revised subtotals).

Reconcile grader/metadata reporting: ensure that top-level passed_criteria and failed_criteria are consistent with rubric_item_scores and with deterministic precheck results. Explicitly separate artifact-contract (presence) results from semantic correctness results in metadata fields (e.g., artifact_contract_passed vs semantic_passed) and add a brief note explaining the distinction.
Untruncate and include the full repair_disposition_table.md content in the revision (or ensure the new calculation_workbook + assumptions file fully capture any text/truncated sensitivity notes).

If you choose to keep manufacturability as 'needs_review' rather than provisional numeric scoring, document that choice clearly and include a short checklist of the missing manufacturability data items required to close the 10% gap (e.g., manufacturing process complexity, need for specialized tooling, cure/wrap times, typical yield/loss rates).

Run the same verification script (node/python snippet used in the trace) as part of the revision and include its output as a support file (agent_trace.json or verification_output.json) to demonstrate reproducibility.
Fix small metadata inconsistencies highlighted by the verifier (see metadata_json.extra_model_fields.verifier_issues) before resubmitting so automated verifiers can pass without ambiguity.
artifact_specific_comments: repair_disposition_table.md: good reproducible arithmetic and clear methods. Add computational workbook (CSV/JSON) and move detailed sensitivity tables into assumptions_and_sensitivity_notes.md to avoid truncation issues.
feedback_summary: The baseline attempt correctly implements packet-bound min-max normalization and weighting for strength, density (mass), and cost, and documents that manufacturability data were not provided. Arithmetic was reproduced in the trace and artifact. However, the submission and associated grader JSON contain internal consistency issues around reporting of the manufacturability rubric item (conflicting pass/fail flags and mixed artifact-presence vs semantic scoring). The artifact preview is truncated which limits full external verification of appended sensitivity text. The semantic treatment of manufacturability is weak: the worker correctly did not invent a manufacturability score but did not supply a provisional qualitative->numeric rubric or a documented fallback assumption and sensitivity sweep for the 10% weight. Also, the verifier flagged the inconsistent grader reporting that should be reconciled in the updated attempt metadata.

verifier_issues.description: Conflicting pass/fail reporting for ri_manufacturability across grader fields (failed_criteria vs passed_criteria vs rubric_item_scores).
verifier_issues.recommended_fix: Unify pass/fail lists and ensure rubric_item_scores.passed matches top-level passed_criteria/failed_criteria. Distinguish artifact-contract pass from semantic pass in metadata.

verifier_issues.description: Deterministic precheck (artifact presence) returned artifact_presence_ok=true for manufacturability while model grader assigned a lower semantic score; the combined grader JSON mixes these results ambiguously.
verifier_issues.recommended_fix: Add explicit fields like artifact_contract_passed and semantic_passed for each rubric item and document their meanings in grader metadata.

verifier_issues.description: Artifact preview truncated; sensitivity and possibly concluding sentences are cut off in the preview which limits external verification.
verifier_issues.recommended_fix: Include full artifact contents or attach separate machine-readable calculation and assumptions artifacts.

extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: Verifier reported internal consistency issues in grader metadata and truncated artifact preview. See individual issues list.
